health_zone_table.basis: SitRep40 Table 2 (23 June 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF and reconciled to Table 1/province totals 1118/291. No new affected health zone is reported; the affected-zone count remains 34/104. The table carries 17 Ituri cases in zones not yet identified; these remain an unventilated residual and are not distributed to named health zones. PDF sha256 cbfe0414399f008c51ac90d7aa3fb552574eb02dd453fe290fa04f2cdb7ff9c3.

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1101 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation; all 291 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep40 section 4.3: Ituri 74 collected/analyzed / 23 positive; Nord-Kivu 76 collected, 39 analyzed / 1 positive; Sud-Kivu 7 collected/analyzed / 0 positive. Positivity uses 24 positives over 120 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: End-of-day isolation stood at 408 patients (192 confirmed, 216 suspected) at 81.0% occupancy.
Ituri isolation occupancy was 91.3% with 337 patients isolated at end day; Nord-Kivu was 57.3% with 59 and Sud-Kivu 37.5% with 12.
No patient escaped during the cycle; 7 recovered exits were recorded. Two recovered PPL were readmitted to CTE Rwampara, one of whom became positive again.
highlights: 24 new confirmed cases including 12 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-23, in Ituri (23 including 12 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (1).
New confirmed cases were distributed across Bunia (8), Mongbwalu (4), Komanda (3), Mangala (3), Nia-Nia (3) and Lita (2) in Ituri, and Butembo (1) in Nord-Kivu.
7 patients were declared recovered in Ituri after negative control tests, bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 122.
laboratory: SitRep40 reports Ituri 74 samples collected and analyzed with 23 positive (31.1%).
Nord-Kivu collected 76 samples, analyzed 39, with 1 positive (2.6%); Sud-Kivu collected and analyzed 7, all negative.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 40 reports through 2026-06-23 and was published on the INSP WordPress site on 2026-06-25T10:09:34Z (post 25050).
The outbreak remains reported across 3 provinces and 34 of 104 health zones; no new affected health zone is reported in this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1020 of 1118 DRC confirmed cases (91.2%).
Nord-Kivu stands at 95 confirmed cases and keeps the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 54.7%, led by Oicha (66.7%), Kyondo (66.7%), Beni (60.0%), and Katwa (59.4%).
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 699 alerts were reported and 695 investigated, producing a 99.4% investigation rate; Sud-Kivu investigated 20 of 24 alerts (83.3%).
138 suspected cases of the day were validated, including 45 deceased validated alerts.
8,346 contacts were under follow-up and 6,435 were seen, producing 77.1% follow-up coverage; Nord-Kivu follow-up (73.3%) is computed on 7 of 10 reporting zones.
307 new contacts were listed across five Ituri health zones; 155 contacts completed 21-day follow-up successfully, including 78 in Sud-Kivu and 77 in Nord-Kivu.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: 307 new contacts were listed across five Ituri health zones; 155 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (78 Sud-Kivu, 77 Nord-Kivu). Nord-Kivu contact follow-up is computed on 7 of 10 reporting zones (70.0% completeness).

challenges.finding: Ituri CTE saturation reached 91.3% with an estimated gap of about 20 isolation centers.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction and expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up reached 77.1% versus the 95% target (a 17.9 pp gap).
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet precisely ventilated by health zone.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, duplicate cleanup, field verification, and reclassification.

challenges.finding: SitRep40 challenge table reports 78 health-worker infections and 18 deaths (CFR 23.1%).
challenges.response: Reinforce IPC/WASH supplies (PPE, chlorine), supervision, and training.

challenges.finding: Nord-Kivu carries a laboratory sample backlog and late diagnosis amid a 54.7% provincial CFR.
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: 307 new contacts were listed across five Ituri health zones; 155 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (78 Sud-Kivu, 77 Nord-Kivu); Nord-Kivu follow-up is computed on 7 of 10 reporting zones (70.0% completeness).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones
Protect Ituri isolation capacity as occupancy stays high (91.3%) and close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers
Improve contact follow-up (77.1% vs 95% target) and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog given the 54.7% provincial CFR

preserved_conflicts: SitRep40 headline is DRC-only (1118 confirmed / 291 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.
SitRep40 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.

Nord-Kivu includes two additional confirmed deaths among previously confirmed cases (one in Kyondo, one in Musienene); they raise the province death total but are not part of the 24h new-confirmed increment.
Daily suspected cases (138) and the 45 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.
The confirmed-count banner carries an asterisk noting DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization and consolidation.
Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard with 2 deaths and 11 recoveries, carried forward unchanged from SitRep39 (INSP reports DRC only).

review_notes: SitRep #040 (report date 2026-06-23; INSP WordPress post 25050 published 2026-06-25T10:09:34Z; embedded PDF media 25052). PDF sha256 cbfe0414399f008c51ac90d7aa3fb552574eb02dd453fe290fa04f2cdb7ff9c3, 11 pages. The PDF cover page shows publication date 24 June 2026; the public WordPress post landed 25 June 2026. Treated as report-date 2026-06-23.

DRC headline 1118 confirmed / 291 confirmed deaths; +24 confirmed and +12 confirmed deaths reported for 2026-06-23 (Ituri 23/12, Nord-Kivu 1/0). Nord-Kivu also recorded 2 additional deaths among previously confirmed cases (Kyondo, Musienene), included in the 291 cumulative.
The affected-zone count remains 34/104. No new affected health zone is reported, so no new map geometry/centroid is required this cycle.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1118 confirmed / 291 deaths: 1101 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with all 291 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.

Recovered advanced to 122 DRC cumulative (+7, all Ituri). Uganda anchor unchanged at 20 cumulative cases (per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard) with 2 deaths and 11 recoveries, so country-scope is 1138 confirmed / 293 deaths / 133 recovered.
Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.3: Ituri 74 collected/analyzed, 23 positive; Nord-Kivu 76 collected, 39 analyzed, 1 positive; Sud-Kivu 7 collected/analyzed, 0 positive.

Operational data captured: 699 alerts (695 investigated, 99.4%; Sud-Kivu 20/24), 138 suspected cases of the day (45 deceased validated alerts), 8,346 contacts under follow-up (77.1%; Nord-Kivu on 7/10 reporting zones), 408 in isolation (192 confirmed / 216 suspected, 81.0% occupancy), 307 new contacts (5 Ituri zones), PoC activation 86.8% (59/68) with 222,806 travelers passed.

Geographic note: Nord-Kivu provincial CFR 54.7% remains the highest, led by Oicha (66.7%), Kyondo (66.7%), Beni (60.0%), Katwa (59.4%); Mongbwalu severity persists at 42.7% (103/241). INSP flags continued progression and possible rapid geographic expansion. Promoted as table facts only.
